message:
fix: harden ext4 fsck group descriptor verification, cap btrfs symlink reads, normalize formatting

Ext4 integrity verification (verify_ext4_integrity):
- Restructure group descriptor verification loop to fail early when
  group_desc_offset() returns None (overflow), rather than silently
  skipping the checksum check after reading the descriptor
- Add explicit bounds check for group descriptor byte range against
  image length with descriptive error messages
- Parse raw group descriptor bytes via Ext4GroupDesc::parse_from_bytes()
  before checksum verification to catch structural parse errors
- Remove the nested match-within-match pattern in favor of flat
  early-continue control flow for clarity

Btrfs symlink readlink:
- Replace unwrap_or(4096) with expect() on PATH_MAX-capped u32 conversion
  since the value is already clamped to LINUX_PATH_MAX (4096) which
  always fits in u32 — panic message documents the invariant
- Add btrfs_readlink_caps_target_at_path_max test that creates a symlink
  exceeding PATH_MAX and verifies readlink truncates to exactly PATH_MAX

Issue tracker updates:
- Close bd-11jp (btrfs symlink PATH_MAX cap) and bd-2cfz (gd_off
  usize::MAX fallback fix) as completed
- Open bd-2b76 for workspace rustfmt drift in ffs-alloc and ffs-ondisk

Formatting normalization (rustfmt):
- ffs-alloc: reflow long try_alloc_inode_in_group_persist call
- ffs-ondisk/ext4: reflow checked_mul extent entry/index offset
  expressions to standard rustfmt line-break style

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
